name: Modern Eagle
about: Modern Eagle is a members-only golf simulator studio offering 24/7 access in a private, country club-like setting. The facility features a single premium hitting bay equipped with a Trackman iO simulator and high-end technology. Members can book tee times through the website or Trackman golf app and access the facility using a digital door link. The club is designed for serious golfers who want to improve their game in a distraction-free environment, with options for monthly memberships or prepaid session cards. There are no initiation fees, and memberships operate on a month-to-month basis that renews automatically until cancelled.
why_golfers_like_it: 24/7 unmanned access provides ultimate flexibility for practice and play
High-end Trackman iO simulator with 4K projector offers precise data and immersive gameplay
Private country-club-like environment ensures a distraction-free experience
No initiation fees and flexible month-to-month membership options

food_and_drink.notes: No mention of on-site food service; members are likely allowed to bring their own.
No mention of alcohol service; the facility is members-only and appears focused on golf.

simulator_hardware.notes: Primary simulator with a top of the line 4k BenQ projector, multiple external monitors, premium impact screen, and Sonos sound system.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: It is assumed that members would need to bring their own clubs, as the venue is a simulator facility and not a club rental service.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: While not explicitly stated, the venue's private, members-only club model suggests that bringing your own food and drinks is likely permitted.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: Standard 18-hole simulator times typically range from 1.5 to 2.5 hours, depending on the simulator software and player speed.

faq.question: Is this venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: No, members must be 21 years or older, so the venue is not kid-friendly.
